message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Hansen’s disease (HD) is endemic in Brazil, a country with the third highest number of COVID-19 cases in the world and the second highest number of COVID-19 deaths. COVID-19 in persons affected by HD has not been described at population level in this country.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    We collated numbers of COVID-19 cases and deaths among patients who were receiving routine treatment for HD at six centres across Brazil (Belém, Bauru, Brasília, Vitória, Petrolina, Palmas) between 1
                    <jats:sup>st</jats:sup>
                    March and 10
                    <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                    December 2020.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Of 1,333 HD patients receiving treatment, 70 (5.2%) reported having had COVID-19. Almost all patients (97% (1,296/1,333)) including all but one of the COVID-19 cases were receiving MDT comprising rifampicin (600mg once per month), dapsone (100mg daily), and clofazimine (50 mg daily plus 300 mg once per month). Four patients died, including a patient in their 30’s on MDT who had a severe type 2 HD reaction (erythema nodosum leprosum) and who was taking clofazimine 100mg daily.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We cannot determine from these preliminary data whether persons affected by Hansen’s disease have a higher or lower risk of COVID-19 and related mortality compared with the general population. We will continue to monitor the effects of COVID-19 in persons affected by and treated for HD and extend this to monitor SARS-CoV-2 vaccine effectiveness in this group of patients.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: COVID-19 in persons affected by Hansen’s disease in Brazil

reference.unstructured: WHO. Weekly epidemiological record Nos 35/36. Geneva: WHO, 2019 30/08/2019.
COVID-19 no Brasil Brasília: Ministério da Saúde,. 2020 [05/12/2020]. Available from: https://susanalitico.saude.gov.br/extensions/covid-19_html/covid-19_html.html.
World Health Organization. WHO multidrug therapy (MDT) Geneva: WHO; 2013 [04/04/2020]. Available from: https://www.who.int/lep/mdt/en/.

reference.article-title: Adverse effects from multi-drug therapy in leprosy: a Brazilian study

reference.unstructured: Opromolla DVA , Dalpino D , Tonello CJS , Fleury RN , Neto JBB . Resultados inicias com clofazimina no tratamento da Hanseníase. Anais Brasileiros da Sociedade Brasileira de Dermatologia. 1972;47(1).

reference.article-title: Personal experience with clofazimine in the treatment of leprosy
reference.journal-title: Anais Brasileiros da Sociedade Brasileira de Dermatologia

Opromolla DVA . Terapêutica multidroga. Anais Brasileiros da Sociedade Brasileira de Dermatologia. 1990;65(1).

reference.article-title: Hemolytic anemia in patients receiving daily dapsone for the treatment of leprosy
